meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Cruise ship outbreak leaves 3 dead as officials delay medical evacuations and probe hantavirus threat
items.summary: A suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship off West Africa has killed three people, with the WHO coordinating emergency medical evacuations.

items.title: Somali pirate and Houthi alliance targets $1T oil trade route with revived hijack tactic
items.summary: Analysts warn of a Red Sea security vacuum as Somali pirates and Houthi-linked groups allegedly collaborate using new tech to hijack oil tankers.

items.title: Cargo ship attacked by small craft near Strait of Hormuz, UK maritime agency says
items.summary: A large cargo ship was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday as Iran pushes to assert control over the vital global energy shipping route.

items.title: Examining NATO: Inside the ‘commitment gap’ as US carries alliance deterrence
items.summary: Trump pressures NATO allies to boost defense spending as experts describe a decades-long commitment gap between political promises and military capability.

items.title: 'Killing off the country': Iran executes dozens, arrests 4,000-plus in war crackdown
items.summary: Iran is being accused of intensifying its crackdown on dissent with executions, mass arrests and one of the internet longest shutdowns to stifle protests against the regime.

items.title: Iran’s killer drones increase slaughter in Sudan amid world’s forgotten war
items.summary: Iran is accused of supplying Mohajer-6 attack drones to Sudan's armed forces as the civil war's death toll reportedly reaches as many as 400,000 people.

items.title: Bus plunges into river after trainee driver crash, massive rescue response: reports
items.summary: All four people on board were rescued after a trainee driver sent a bus careening into the River Seine near Paris Thursday, officials said.

items.title: May Day protests across Europe and Asia turn into anti-American, anti-Israel political battlegrounds
items.summary: May Day demonstrations across multiple continents escalated into clashes as traditional labor protests increasingly became vehicles for anti-war and anti-Israel political movements.

items.title: North Korea's extreme battlefield doctrine revealed by Kim Jong Un during speech
items.summary: Kim Jong Un publicly praised North Korean soldiers who chose self-destruction over capture in Ukraine War, marking a stark confirmation of battlefield policy.

items.title: Trump’s 'Economic Fury' squeezes Iran — but can Tehran outlast the pressure?
items.summary: Trump's escalating sanctions, naval blockade and financial enforcement against Iran test whether economic strain can force Iran's regime to bend.

items.title: Global famine fears rise as Hormuz crisis threatens ‘eight-year,' Suez-scale disruption
items.summary: Global famine fears are rising as the Strait of Hormuz crisis strains food supply chains, drives up fertilizer prices and threatens harvests worldwide.

items.title: Mother-in-law of slain beauty queen arrested after international manhunt
items.summary: Authorities arrested the suspected fugitive mother-in-law accused of killing a former Mexican beauty queen after a two-week international manhunt.

items.title: Britain warns another terror attack is 'highly likely' within 6 months after London stabbing
items.summary: Britain raised its national terror threat level to severe after two people were stabbed in an antisemitic attack in London's Golders Green neighborhood.

items.title: Israel defense chief warns strikes on Iran could resume soon, signals campaign not over
items.summary: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz warns military action against Iran may resume soon, saying Tehran has suffered severe blows throughout the past year.

items.title: Rubio warns China after Panama ship detentions, calls hemisphere sovereignty 'non-negotiable'
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio warns hemisphere sovereignty is "non-negotiable" as U.S. and allies condemn China's reported detention of Panama ships.

items.title: ‘No credibility’: Obama’s top Iran negotiator torched by State Department after ripping Trump war plan
items.summary: The State Department pushes back on former Obama Iran deal negotiator Wendy Sherman after she criticizes Trump’s strategy, igniting a sharp war of words over Democrats' foreign policy with regime.

items.title: Iran’s $800M oil smuggling scheme uses tankers posing as Iraqi ships to dodge blockade
items.summary: Sanctioned tankers are allegedly disguising Iranian oil shipments as Iraqi cargo while Trump doubles down on the naval blockade to squeeze Tehran.

items.title: Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report
items.summary: Human rights groups allege Russia recruits tens of thousands of vulnerable migrants from over 130 countries to fight in its ongoing war against Ukraine.

items.title: Leaked audio shocker: UK envoy says US ‘special relationship’ not with Britain, but another ally
items.summary: Britain's ambassador to Washington reportedly said America's special relationship is probably with Israel, igniting diplomatic controversy in London.

items.title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism
items.summary: Two men were stabbed in north London's Barnet borough, prompting a counterterrorism-led investigation into a potentially antisemitic incident.

items.title: Record antisemitic incidents in Canada fuel criticism of Carney government response
items.summary: Canada faces record antisemitic incidents in 2025 as critics say Prime Minister Mark Carney's government is failing to adequately address anti-Jewish hate.

items.title: Court sentences former world leader to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest, other charges
items.summary: Ousted South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to seven years in prison after martial law decree that triggered a severe political crisis.

items.title: UN-backed data undercuts viral Gaza famine claims as child malnutrition falls
items.summary: United Nations data challenges viral Gaza famine claims as experts say Hamas is weaponizing humanitarian narratives to undermine a post-war coalition.

items.title: Tourist dies at luxury resort after cobra from snake show climbs up pants, bites him: police
items.summary: A tourist reportedly died near a luxury all-inclusive resort complex after a venomous cobra slithered up his pants and bit him during a snake show, police said.

items.title: New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah
items.summary: The New IRA reportedly claimed a car bomb attack on a Belfast police station, threatening further strikes amid alleged Iran and Hezbollah connections.
